key_insight: LLM judges that score synthetic math solutions well can still fail badly on real student reasoning, exposing a large evaluation gap.
engineering_relevance: For automated grading and math-eval pipelines, benchmarks built only on synthetic outputs may overestimate real-world reliability and lead to poor deployment decisions.
method: Built RealMath-Eval with 224 annotated high-school exam responses.
Compared state-of-the-art LLM judges on real human answers versus synthetic LLM-generated solutions.
Used embedding analysis, surprisal probes, and style-transfer tests to explain the gap.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports expert-alignment error around MSE ~2.96 on real student responses versus ~1.17 on synthetic solutions.
watch_item: The benchmark is relatively small and domain-specific, so results may not generalize across subjects, grade levels, or grading rubrics.
paper_title: RealMath-Eval: Why SOTA Judges Struggle with Real Human Reasoning
